BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

init
Creates the local .brik workspace manifest and append-only evidence ledger.
Example:
  brik64 init

Common parser profile:
  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }
  Core monomer calls use MC_XX.NAME(args). Effectful or non-scalar monomers fail closed unless a boundary exists.

BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

Checks manifest policy, PCD inventory, and PCD parse health.
Example:
  brik64 doctor --scope project --json

  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }
  Core monomer calls use MC_XX.NAME(args). Effectful or non-scalar monomers fail closed unless a boundary exists.

status=pre_public_candidate

certify <file.pcd>
Writes a local candidate certificate beside the PCD.
Example:

  brik64 certify pcd/order_gate.pcd

  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }

BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

emit <file.pcd> --target <ts|rust|python> --out <dir|file> [--tests]
Emits executable local code only after a local candidate certificate exists.
Example:
  brik64 certify pcd/order_gate.pcd
  brik64 emit pcd/order_gate.pcd --target ts --out out-ts --tests

Common parser profile:
  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }
  Core monomer calls use MC_XX.NAME(args). Effectful or non-scalar monomers fail closed unless a boundary exists.

BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

polymerize <files.pcd...> [--local] [--inline] [--root <fn>] --out <file> [--json]
Default local mode writes a root_dag_reference polymer. --inline writes all source functions into one PC.
Example:
  brik64 polymerize pcd/a.pcd pcd/b.pcd --inline --root run_pipeline --out polymer.pcd

  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }

Checks brik64.com release metadata. --install updates only supported ~/.brik64/bin installs after SHA verification.
Example:

BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

ledger <status|verify|append|snapshot|tombstone|export|repair> [options]
Maintains the local .brik append-only evidence ledger. Events are hash-chained and never deleted.
Example:
  brik64 ledger status --json
  brik64 ledger append --type note --ref pcd/order_gate.pcd
  brik64 ledger tombstone <eventHash> --reason superseded
  brik64 ledger export --redacted

Common parser profile:
  PC name { fn name(input: i64) -> i64 { if (input > 0) return input; return 0; } }
  Core monomer calls use MC_XX.NAME(args). Effectful or non-scalar monomers fail closed unless a boundary exists.

lock [--files <a.pcd,b.pcd>|<files...>] [--skip-errors] [--json]
Writes brik64.lock.json for valid PCDs. Default mode fails closed on the first parse error.
Example:
  brik64 lock --files pcd/a.pcd,pdc/b.pcd --skip-errors --json

BRIK64 CLI 0.1.0-beta.15.5
status=pre_public_candidate

help exit-codes
Shows process exit code meanings for CI and scripts.
  0  success
  2  unknown command
  64  policy or option gate
  65  PCD parse or validation error
  66  file not found
  67  certificate required
  68  hash mismatch
  69  managed endpoint unavailable
  70  internal guarded error
  73  output exists
  74  filesystem error
